key_points: Resources are substances or materials that have utility and value for humans
A material becomes a resource when it is technologically accessible, economically feasible, and culturally acceptable
Natural resources are classified as renewable and non-renewable based on their ability to regenerate
Human resources include people’s knowledge, skills, health, and ability to develop resources
Resource conservation and sustainable development are important to prevent depletion of natural resources
examples: Natural resources: Soil for agriculture, minerals for industries
Human-made resources: Machines, buildings, roads, and bridges
Human resources: Skilled engineers, teachers, scientists
Renewable resources: Wind energy, solar energy, forests
Non-renewable resources: Coal, petroleum, and natural gas
important_terms: Resource Development: The process of using resources for human benefit through technology and skills
Renewable Resources: Resources that can be replenished naturally in a short time
Non-renewable Resources: Resources that take millions of years to form and cannot be quickly replaced
Resource Conservation: Careful use and management of resources to avoid depletion
Sustainable Development: Development that meets present needs without harming future generations
exam_tips: Understand how technology helps convert natural materials into useful resources
Remember the classification of resources and examples for each type
Learn the difference between renewable and non-renewable resources with examples
Explain the concept of sustainable development with practical examples
Focus on the importance of resource conservation in protecting the environment
